# Walk-Forward Out-of-Sample Future Hypervolume — paper headline (proper methodology)

*Generated by `python_refactor/experiments/walk_forward_report.py` (W14-2).*
*Implements thesis §7.2.2 rolling-period protocol — see [`docs/THESIS-INDEX.md`](THESIS-INDEX.md).*

## Protocol (thesis §7.2.3)

- **Train window**: 378 business days (~1.5 years)
- **Step**: 50 business days (~2.5 months)
- **MC scenarios per period (E)**: 200
- **Reference point z_ref**: (0.2, 0.0) — risk_max=0.2, return_min=0.0
- **Scenarios**: S0, S2
- **Seeds**: 2
- **Wall-clock**: 2470.0s

## Aggregated results (per scenario, across seeds × periods)

| scenario | n_seeds | grand mean Ŝ | std | median | min | max |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| S0 | 2 | 0.000252735 | 1.01916e-05 | 0.000252735 | 0.000245529 | 0.000259942 |
| S2 | 2 | 0.000189895 | 1.18081e-05 | 0.000189895 | 0.000181545 | 0.000198244 |

## Headline observation (paper §V-D claim direction)

**S2 (anticipatory) grand mean Ŝ = 0.000189895**
**S0 (myopic) grand mean Ŝ = 0.000252735**

Delta (S2 − S0) = **-6.28406e-05** (-24.86%); Welch t = **-5.7**, df ≈ 1. Paper claim direction (Table 7.2): S2 > S0. Empirical direction here: **S2 ≤ S0 (inconsistent — W15 candidate)**.
